summary: Build tag_concepts.py (LLM-assisted tagging with structured scoring prompt)
description:
## Goal

Build `tag_concepts.py` — Pass B of Stage 3. For each chunk, ask an LLM to score it against the concept taxonomy and write proposals to `staged_tags`.

## File Location

`scripts/tag_concepts.py`

## CLI

```
tag_concepts.py --provider {ollama|anthropic|openai} --model <name> [--batch-size N] [--resume] [--tradition X] [--text Y]
```

## Responsibilities

1. Load taxonomy from `concepts/taxonomy.toml`.
2. For each chunk (filtered by tradition/text if given), build the structured scoring prompt (see arch doc §4.6.1 + ticket subtask 3.4.4).
3. Send to the configured provider (ticket subtask 3.4.1 — provider abstraction).
4. Parse the structured response and upsert into `staged_tags`. Concepts not in the taxonomy go in with `is_new_concept=true` and a `new_concept_def`.
5. Track progress in `tagging_progress` so `--resume` skips completed chunks.
6. Honor `--batch-size` for rate-limit pacing.

## Subtasks (from doc)

- Provider abstraction (ollama / anthropic / openai)
- Progress tracking + `--resume`
- Batch-size rate limiting
- Structured scoring prompt template

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every chunk has at least one `staged_tags` row after a full run
- `--resume` correctly skips completed chunks
- Provider can be swapped via flag
- Scoring output is structured (parseable score 0-3 + justification)

resolution.note: scripts/tag_concepts.py: structured scoring prompt, 3 provider backends (ollama/anthropic/openai), JSON response parsing, staged_tags upsert, tagging_progress for --resume, --batch-size rate limiting.
